other_observers: No one else was with me at the time of initial discovery. COBIRDS reports were submitted by others that saw these birds afterwords. Paul Differding and Charlie Schmucker saw the immature bird with me on the 27th as part of my monthly Chatfield Birdwalk that I do for State Parks.; I am the original finder.
specific_location: Chatfield Reservoir; Chatfield State Park; Birds seen out in the water from the marina "sand spit".
description: Adult: small loon with thin bill; lower mandible slightly upturned at the gonys. Head small and rounded (not blocky like a COLO or YBLO); Face, chin, throat, and chest white with a clear, straight line of demarcation on the side of the neck between the white front of the neck and the grayish crown and hind neck. Eyes were visible in the white portion of the face. Head and beak held above horizontal. Back dark (almost black) with white flecking;  Immature: small loon with thin bill; lower mandible ?upturned? at the gonys. Head small and rounded (not blocky like a COLO or YBLO); Front, sides and rear of neck dirty tan fading to a light tan at the front of the neck; front of neck with a small patch of reddish-brown coloration in the center of the neck; chin white.Behaviors: Both loons were found swimming and diving together along the face of the dam.  Adult was seen November 10-27, 2010 Immature was seen by me only on November 10, 2010Call: No vocalizations were heardPlumage: 1-adult basic, 1-first basic
